item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Disembodied Asshole has left the channel (reason: total butthurt)

+1

item.title: Re: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded: Glad you enjoyed him, I certainly enjoyed writing him more than any other character I've written here.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
This was requested on Site-42 so I put it together, and I'm now a big fan of Saturn Deer. Hadn't met him before this, so thanks!

https://youtu.be/y37TUGT4rkI

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meet Saturn Deer, cosmic troll.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I thought it could be Mary Nakayama from SCP-0001 but then you came with this "pulse" thing.

Help to catch up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I apologize, it's not my best work (recorded two years ago), but I made a ready-read of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I happen to have done some research on the subject…

And the lesser kestrel does indeed have cute fluffy feathers.

It's rare that I get a laugh like this from this site. And in a mainlist article too. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What did i read. +1

Given all the anomalies the Foundation contains that have the capability to affect records, i wonder how often something like this — well, kind of like this — happens. Ordinary day, you're checking the containment records for your newest assignment, and realize it's been rewritten to something else1 sigh and call whatever branch cleans up this kind of thing. Either that or start seizing and choking on your tongue from a cognitohazard.

Footnotes
1. This is the part where i'd place a humorous example of what i mean, if i were funny enough to do so. I am not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found this really funny.

Disembodied Asshole has left the channel (reason: total butthurt)

HA! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In-joke? Not really. A reference to the Saturn Deer things and a few other tales? Yes.

As for why it's an SCP, I explained my reasoning on the previous page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it and think this is much preferable as an SCP than as a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was just kind of confusing - perhaps I'm missing some subtle reference or in-joke.

Though TBH, I'm rather sick of SCPs diverging from their documentations to tell stories. It was novel when 2718 did it, but now…eh. Your writing is fine, but I've seen this particular trick done one too many times to give it a pass yet again. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was all set to novote this. The Pulse comes off like every wangsty adolescent male power fantasy ever. Then suddenly Saturn Deer. And he called the Pulse out on his BS and with all the mockery and disdain he deserved and I couldn't not upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love every word of this. Yet another time when I'm a little sad I can only upvote once.

edit: Also, this may be one of the greatest lines I have ever read:

How dare you?! Insolent wretch! I will have the skin ripped from your back, your bones shattered, you will not be invited to my pretty princess tea party, will receive no crumpets, I-

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, much obliged. I'll get on fixing those shortly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Goddammit, Saturn Deer. I don't know if this is good, but I loved it.

Typos
For your perusal

I didn't make any changes because, aside from one or two of these, I wasn't sure if maybe the mistakes were intentional.

has began

begun

bit bit bit

Usually "bit by bit".

I cannot allow to to interfere

Should that be "allow it"?

You have no idea who I am, don't you.

"do you", also needs a question mark

can wreck havoc

wreak

20,000,000$

Usually the dollar sign goes in front of the dollar amount.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are several elements to this, as I see it:

First is the simple fact of surprise. If you start reading a tale and see the standard SCP formatting, you know something is up- there is going to be one sort of twist or the other. In a mainlister, the sudden changes are different from the norm, making the shift much more effective than in a tale.

Second is the way the narrative of the article works- a tale is usually viewed as something happening *outside* the base reality set in the main list. This article depends on the Pulse interrupting this very same safe base reality, and Saturn Deer doing the same to it in return. This impression simply would not have worked in a tale.

Third is the matter of perspective. Tales only very rarely appeal to the reader in the very direct way any SCP does- in an SCP the reader is the direct receiver of all information given in the article, unlike in tales where there is typically a mediator of some sorts. As illustrated in the previous point, this sort of direct perspective is crucial to the way this particular article works, or at least to how I intended it to work.

Djoric did indeed do a similar thing, but his article was based far more on direct narrative than this one, which is more about meta trickery and perspective manipulation. It is a good example for what it does, but what it does is not the same as what this article does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Until I realized that this was Saturn Deer, I wasn't very sold on the idea.

I wonder if that speaks more about Saturn Deer or the idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, but at what point does it stop being an SCP mainlister and start being a story using the SCP format as an excuse to tell it?

Djoric did a similar thing, and that turned out beautifully as a tale. And again, I find the writing here excellent, but by my standards, I downvoted.

I am curious about how this wouldn't work as a tale. Is it the mere fact that it'd be labeled as a tale that'd ruin it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And here I was, trying to write some guy who got stuck in an SCP entry, and then this comes along! How does one compete?!

Oh, and you are the supreme overlord of meta. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really want to love this and FWIW I like enough of it too much to downvote. Maybe I'll even upvote it later. A lot of it is fun.

But it feels like it needs a few more editing passes. It goes on too long (in the beginning/middle stages — I almost quit reading before Saturn Deer showed up and it started being entertaining again) and some of it feels forced. I think cleanup would improve this to the stage of easy upvote for me.

I'll try to re-read it later, see if I have more specific feedback.

That aside, I strongly disagree that this should be a tale. There's nothing about this whatsoever that makes it not an SCP. There is no such SCP/Tale line except for personal aesthetic preference; while I can't fault anyone's personal aesthetic preference, I do not share this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me a lot of SCP-2413, except I liked the format screw, context, and dialogue better in that one.

Downsides, it ran long for a format screw and the baddie got rather melodramatic.
Upsides, his comeuppance was rather amusing. I enjoy Saturn Deer, but it's been a while and I had to think back to get the context.

Ultimately, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yep, fixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You will release your wards to pray on the world

Typo?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this absolutely wouldn't work as a tale, and is a wonderful example of just how far we can bend the SCP format.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's fair enough. I realized this would probably happen when I wrote the thing, and you indeed gave me your thoughts on this previous to me posting it. Still, I think that it is best where it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've said my thoughts in chat, but I'll reiterate here:

I love this, and think it's really well-done and executed well… but ultimately, this plunges over the line from "SCP that tells a story" to "full-on tale", and I'd rather upvote this as a tale. This is the same reason why I downvoted 2718 and, shit, that's a fuckload more conventional than this. So while the writing is impeccable, and in-universe I can believe this to be a thing, I'm downvoting this for consistency of my own standards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Brilliance. Sheer, utter brilliance.

Dmatix, why you so epic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one is a rather unusual usage of the SCP format, I'm well aware, but one I believe fits it better than posting this article as a tale. I enjoyed writing it a great deal.
